title: Eleusine lagopoides
intro: Eleusine lagopoides, also known as Snake's Beard or Snake's Grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Eleusine lagopoides is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Eleusine lagopoides is classified as a species of grass within the genus Eleusine, which belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass that is native to tropical Africa. The genus Eleusine is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which contains several species of grasses. Eleusine lagopoides is further classified as a member of the tribe Cynodonteae, a group of grasses that are primarily found in tropical regions. The classification of Eleusine lagopoides is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Tracheophyta, class Liliopsida, order Poales, family Poaceae, genus Eleusine, species E. lagopoides.
